skill.name: thinking-partner
skill.description: 思考拍档 - 陪你从混沌中理清局面，锁定核心问题，拆解卡点，共创解法，落地行动
skill.summary: A structured thinking partner that guides users through a five-step process to clarify problems, identify root causes, and create actionable plans
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The skill is a text-based coaching framework with no code execution, network access, or filesystem operations. Line 33 markdown code blocks were misidentified as shell execution. Line 3 YAML frontmatter was misidentified as cryptographic content. Line 1 YAML delimiters were misidentified as obfuscation. No security risks detected.

content.user_title: Transform Complex Problems Into Clear Action Plans
content.value_statement: When facing confusing situations, you need structured thinking not quick answers. This skill guides you through a proven five-step process to identify core problems, uncover root causes, and create executable plans.
actual_capabilities: Guide users through a structured five-step problem solving process
Ask targeted questions to gather relevant context and background
Help identify the core problem among multiple competing issues
Break down problems to find root causes through layered analysis
Facilitate solution brainstorming based on user's own insights
Create actionable plans with clear priorities and boundaries
limitations: Cannot execute code, access files, or make external API calls
Relies entirely on information the user chooses to share
Cannot make decisions for users - only facilitates their thinking
Not suitable for emergencies requiring immediate professional intervention

use_cases.title: Career Decision Support
use_cases.description: Work through complex career choices like job changes, role transitions, or work-life balance challenges by identifying what truly matters to you.
use_cases.target_user: Professionals facing career crossroads or workplace challenges

use_cases.title: Business Problem Analysis
use_cases.description: Break down business challenges such as product direction, team dynamics, or growth strategies by separating signals from noise.
use_cases.target_user: Founders, product managers, and team leaders

use_cases.title: Personal Goal Planning
use_cases.description: Transform vague aspirations into concrete action plans by clarifying motivations, identifying obstacles, and designing sustainable steps.
use_cases.target_user: Individuals pursuing personal development or life changes

prompt_templates.title: Quick Problem Clarification
prompt_templates.prompt: I am facing a situation where [describe your situation]. My main goal is [what you want to achieve]. What I have tried so far is [your attempts]. Help me identify what the real problem is.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you have a specific issue but feel stuck or confused about where to start

prompt_templates.title: Career Crossroads Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: I am at a career crossroads. Currently I work as [your role] at [company type]. I am considering [option A] vs [option B]. My priorities are [list 2-3 key factors]. Walk me through the five-step thinking process to reach clarity.
prompt_templates.scenario: When facing a significant career decision with multiple options

prompt_templates.title: Business Strategy Deep Dive
prompt_templates.prompt: Our business faces [describe the challenge]. We have [resources/constraints]. The team is divided on [specific disagreement]. I need you to act as a thinking partner and help me work through this systematically without jumping to solutions.
prompt_templates.scenario: For leaders who need structured thinking on complex business problems

prompt_templates.title: Life Planning Session
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to make progress on [life area: health/relationships/learning/etc.]. Right now I feel [current state]. My ideal outcome is [vision]. However I keep getting stuck because [obstacles]. Guide me through a complete thinking session from problem identification to action plan.
prompt_templates.scenario: For comprehensive personal development planning sessions

output_examples.input: User says: I am overwhelmed with too many projects and do not know what to focus on
output: I hear you. Let us start by understanding your situation. First, what projects are on your plate right now? List the top 3-5 that are causing the most stress.
After listing: Good. Now for each project, what is the expected outcome and by when? This helps us see what truly matters.
Based on what you shared, I notice a pattern. The core issue seems to be [identified pattern]. Does this resonate with you?

output_examples.input: User says: My team disagrees on product direction and I need to make a decision
output: Let us map this out. What are the different directions being proposed? And what evidence or reasoning supports each view?
I see. Before we evaluate options, let me ask: what would success look like six months from now? What metrics or outcomes would tell you this decision was right?
Now let us identify the core question. Is this about market fit, resource allocation, or team alignment? Based on your context, I believe the core issue is [analysis]. What is your reaction to this?

best_practices: Share honest context about your situation - the more真实 information provided, the more targeted the thinking process becomes
Trust the process and complete each step before moving forward - skipping steps leads to superficial conclusions
Speak in your own words rather than trying to use frameworks - clarity comes from authentic expression
anti_patterns: Asking for quick answers without doing the thinking work - this skill facilitates your thinking, it does not replace it
Jumping between multiple unrelated problems in one session - focus on one core challenge per conversation
Ignoring the structured process and demanding immediate solutions - the five-step workflow exists for a reason

faq.question: How is this different from just asking for advice?
faq.answer: Traditional advice gives you answers. This skill helps you discover your own answers through structured questioning. Research shows that solutions you generate yourself are far more likely to be implemented successfully.

faq.question: How long does a complete session take?
faq.answer: A full five-step session typically takes 20-40 minutes depending on problem complexity. However you can also do shorter focused sessions on specific stages like problem identification or action planning.

faq.question: Can I use this for technical problems?
faq.answer: This skill excels at strategic, career, business, and personal problems. For technical debugging or code-specific issues, other specialized skills would be more appropriate.

faq.question: What if I disagree with the thinking partner's analysis?
faq.answer: Disagreement is welcomed and expected. The skill will explain its reasoning and you can challenge it. Often the most valuable insights emerge from these productive tensions.

faq.question: Do I need to share personal or sensitive information?
faq.answer: Share only what you are comfortable sharing. The quality of thinking correlates with context quality, but you control what information to provide. Use abstract descriptions if needed.

faq.question: Can I save the session output for later reference?
faq.answer: Yes. At the end of any session, you can ask the skill to summarize the key decisions, insights, and action items into a reference document you can save and revisit.
